sentence_reviews.text: Nodes, edges, paths, spectra, and connectivity support relational learning, search, and structured knowledge.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Chapter 1.2.2 'Mathematics' (line 28); Chapter 3 'Solving Problems by Searching' (lines 68-101); Chapter 6 'Constraint Satisfaction Problems' (lines 148-170); Chapter 7.4-7.5 'Propositional Logic: A Very Simple Logic' and 'Propositional Theorem Proving' (lines 177-189); Chapter 13.5 'Causal Networks' (lines 356-359); Chapter 15.1 'Relational Probability Models' (lines 384-387); Chapter 20.3 'Learning with Hidden Variables: The EM Algorithm' (lines 553-558).

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Discrete mathematics & logic.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Chapter 1.2.2 'Mathematics' (line 28); Chapter 27.1.1 'The argument from informality' (line 738); Chapter 27.1.3 'The mathematical objection' (lines 736-753); Appendix A: Mathematical Background (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports the general caution that formal mathematics has assumptions and limits, but the empirical-mismatch wording and the dependency clause are atlas-level synthesis.
